compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator abandoned dso-setup.sh fix without investigating root cause of test failures
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed Behavior
The orchestrator attempted 5 different approaches to fix dso-setup.sh line 200 (the last plugins/dso reference). Each approach broke 4 prereq tests. Instead of investigating WHY the tests failed, the orchestrator reverted each attempt and eventually declared the fix 'needs more care' and deferred it.

## Rationalization
The orchestrator rationalized giving up with: 'the fix needs more care to avoid breaking the installer test suite' and 'this is getting complex.' It treated repeated failure as evidence of inherent difficulty rather than investigating the specific failure mechanism.

## Root Cause of the Test Failures
The actual root cause was trivial: `dirname` is at `/usr/bin/dirname` on macOS, not `/bin/dirname`. The prereq tests use `PATH="$fake_dir:/bin"` which excludes `/usr/bin`. The orchestrator's fix used `$(dirname "$0")` which requires the external `dirname` command. The fix was to use `${0%/*}` (bash parameter expansion) instead — a one-character conceptual change.

## Investigation Steps That Were Not Taken
1. Check what PATH the test uses — visible in the test source
2. Check where `dirname` lives on the system — `which dirname`
3. Recognize that `/usr/bin/dirname` is excluded by the test's PATH
4. Use bash parameter expansion `${0%/*}` instead of the external `dirname` command

All four steps are straightforward and would have resolved the issue in under 2 minutes. Instead, the orchestrator spent ~15 minutes trying increasingly complex workarounds (variable placement, fallbacks, pre-set-eu declarations) without ever asking 'why does this specific command fail under this specific PATH?'

## Fix Direction
When a fix breaks tests, the orchestrator should: (1) read the test to understand what environment it creates, (2) reproduce the exact failure condition, (3) identify which command fails and why, (4) fix the specific failing command. Not: try a different approach, revert, try another, revert, give up.

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test
